commit.message:
Fix Amplify build: lazy-initialize Cognito JWT verifier

The CognitoJwtVerifier was created eagerly at module scope, causing
build-time validation errors when amplify_outputs.json contains a
placeholder User Pool ID during the Amplify build phase.

commit.message:
feat: log progress when Karpenter is provisioning a GPU node (#172)

* feat: log progress when Karpenter is provisioning a GPU node during helm install

When `helm install --wait` stalls because no GPU node is available yet,
the install appears completely frozen for 4–8 minutes with no feedback.
This adds a concurrent polling watcher that detects FailedScheduling events
with `nvidia.com/gpu` resource pressure and logs a single warning message
so users know the wait is expected rather than a hang.

The watcher degrades silently if the k8s API is unavailable and is
cancelled cleanly once the helm subprocess completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: harden GPU scheduling watcher after red-team review

- Use asyncio.create_task() over the deprecated ensure_future()
- Suppress both Exception and CancelledError when awaiting the watcher
  in the finally block: CancelledError is BaseException in Python 3.8+,
  so suppress(Exception) alone would let it escape, spuriously triggering
  the install cancellation/uninstall path
- Rephrase warning to avoid attributing the delay to Karpenter specifically
  and drop the hardcoded 4–8 minute estimate
- Add unit tests for _watch_for_scheduling_events: GPU event logs once,
  non-GPU event is ignored, wrong-release event is ignored, k8s client
  failure exits gracefully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add CHANGELOG entry for GPU scheduling watcher

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Art O Cathain <153507562+art-dsit@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(recruit): correct Jacob's voice placement and visual treatment

Behavioral economics review (Freya) identified three issues:

1. Placement: moved from after Character to BETWEEN Coach Monologue 2
   and Character. The coach asks "will he fit our locker room?" →
   Jacob answers → Character section validates → coach decides.

2. Typewriter removed: the typewriter is the coach's voice throughout
   the page. Using it for Jacob collapsed speaker differentiation.
   Now uses CSS fade-in with staggered paragraph delays.

3. "In His Own Words" label removed: it signaled someone else
   presenting him. The oversized faded quotation mark and the
   absence of a container ARE the design signal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

present-tense vulnerability ("I still hear it sometimes"), and a

I wasn't done."). Attribution changed to "Jacob, age 15" per Flow's
recommendation — first name + age signals youth and self-awareness
without the formality of a press release.

feat(recruit): add Jacob's voice, elevate car-ride moment, close coach arc

Three narrative additions that address the top gaps from the 8.4/10
sports psychology review:

1. Car-ride elevation — the age-12 "don't ever let me stop" moment
   pulled out of the timeline into a standalone visual beat with
   typewriter delivery and its own GSAP animation

2. Jacob's Voice section — first-person perspective from the athlete
   himself, positioned after Character. Covers freshman doubt,
   the varsity weight room, and forward momentum. Content refined
   by Flow (sports psychology) to sound authentically 15, not
   ghostwritten.

3. 3rd Coach Monologue — closes the narrative arc after Jacob speaks:
   "Self-made. Coachable. The kid just told me who he is.
   Get him on campus."

Also: lazy loading on film bar poster images (Marcus UX rec).

commit.message:
fix: groups UI, sandwich menu reliability, back button touch

Group UI redesign (presala + quick-add):
- Removed unreliable checkbox+hidden-div pattern entirely
- Enemy setup row now has a direct «Grupo (nº)» input always visible;
  value ≥ 2 makes it a group (PG = HP por miembro, totalHp = PG × nº)
- Quick-add modal (combat) gets the same inline group size input
- addSetupNpc/submitQuickNpc updated to read groupSize directly (no checkbox)
- Hint text: «Nº miembros ≥ 2 → grupo (PG = HP/miembro)»

Sandwich menu (☰) reliability:
- Added _closeOptionsMenu() helper called by all menu buttons
  (no more long inline getElementById chains that can fail)
- setupCombatOptionsMenu: guard with data-menuBound to prevent
  duplicate addEventListener on repeated init() calls
- Document listener now uses capture phase to run reliably
- Dropdown z-index: 300 → 1200 (above combat-body panels)
- Dropdown button padding enlarged for bigger tap target
- touch-action: manipulation on the ☰ toggle button

Back button («← Volver al combate»):
- closeCombatLogView: guard if combat inactive, try/catch renderCombatManager
- touch-action: manipulation + :active feedback on .clv-back-btn
- Larger padding (10px 20px)
- .combat-toolbar gets position:relative + z-index:100 so dropdown is
  never clipped by the combat-body below

Cache: v=20260310n → v=20260310o

- Agent Option 系列（WithLLM/WithTools/WithSystemPrompt 等 10 个选项）

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(procedure): strengthen requirements review with domain grounding, leakage detection, and remediation boundaries

The requirements review procedure was passing artifacts with taxonomy violations,
implementation leakage, and scope creep because it validated structure without
loading domain-specific specs. Four changes:

1. Mandatory domain-context loading before validation
2. Concrete implementation leakage signals (paths, fields, counts, types)
3. DoD gates as systematic review-awareness checklist
4. Auto-remediation limited to fixing existing content, not adding scope

Also cleans up telec-init-enrichment requirements to remove implementation
prescriptions identified during this analysis.

🤖 Generated with [TeleClaude](https://github.com/InstruktAI/TeleClaude)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Section 12: Two-tier Mac photo storage + NSCache + migration tool

Problem: every imported photo was saved as a single 2400 px JPEG (~1.5-3 MB).
MacEntryThumbnail loaded the full-size file from disk on every render with no
caching, making 100 entries ~200 MB and list scrolling disk-bound.

12a — MacImageStore: two-file save
- Add MacImagePair struct { thumbnailFilename, displayFilename }.
- Add saveImagePair(data:) which writes a 400 px / 0.70q thumbnail AND a 1200 px
  / 0.75q display image from a single CGImageSource; returns the pair.
- Add saveResized(source:maxPixel:quality:) private helper shared by both sizes.
- Add deleteFile(filename:) for clean deletion.
- Keep saveDownsampledJPEG as a @available(deprecated) wrapper so no call sites
  break until explicitly migrated.
- MacThumbnail now calls loadThumbnail(for:) instead of loadImage(for:), so list
  views use the 400 px file; detail views keep calling loadImage(for:) for the
  1200 px display image.

12b — MacImportEntries: use saveImagePair on import
- importFromURLs replaces saveDownsampledJPEG with saveImagePair and sets both
  photoFilename (display) and photoThumbnailFilename (thumbnail) on new entries.

12c — MacThumbnailCache (new file)
- NSCache<NSString, NSImage> capped at 200 images / 50 MB.
- MacImageStore.loadThumbnail(for:) checks cache first; stores on first load;
  falls back gracefully to photoFilename for legacy entries with no thumbnail.

12d — EntryStore.cleanupEntry: delete both files on macOS
- Was: delete only photoFilename via raw FileManager.
- Now: MacImageStore.deleteFile(f) for both photoFilename and
  photoThumbnailFilename so no orphaned files accumulate.

12e — SettingsView: Re-compress photos (Mac only)
- New "Re-compress photos" button in the Storage card, hidden on iOS.
- Finds all entries where photoThumbnailFilename == nil && photoFilename != nil,
  regenerates both sizes from the existing file via saveImagePair, updates the
  entry in-place (triggers auto-save via didSet), deletes the old oversized file,
  and invalidates the NSCache. Shows a progress counter while running.
- Entry updates use firstIndex(where: { $0.id == … }) rather than captured indices
  to stay correct if entries shift during the run.

TrailEntry: add photoThumbnailFilename: String? = nil
- Default nil ensures backward-compatible JSON decoding for all existing entries.

Storage comparison (per entry):
  Before: ~2 MB (one 2400 px file)
  After:  ~460 KB (400 px + 1200 px)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Discussion flag button on card detail pages
- Todo drag-to-reorder (up/down arrows with optimistic UI)
- Prev/next card navigation within category
- Sibling position indicator (Card 3 of 8)
- Briefing mode as default view on browse workstream pages
  (key developments, open actions, waiting on external, category summary)
- View toggle (Briefing | Cards) on workstream pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add OmniFocus Raycast extension with Quick Add Anywhere command

Custom OmniFocus integration that lets you fuzzy search all projects
and tasks, then create a task or subtask at the selected destination.
Uses JXA (JavaScript for Automation) for OmniFocus communication.
Supports name, tags, due date, repeat rules, flag, and notes.

commit.message:
feat: add enemy groups + summon system to combat manager

- Enemy groups: toggle in setup + quick-add forms; groupSize × hpPerMember
  aggregate HP; applyGroupDamage() with exact ceil-based member tracking;
  turn queue shows member counter (X/N) and 👥 badge
- Summons: toggle + summoner select (Asthor/Zero) in setup + quick-add ally
  forms; _insertPreCombatSummons() repositions pre-combat summons right after
  their summoner in initiative order; ✨ badge in turn queue
- Zero one-summon rule: enforced on both frontend (fast-path) and backend
  (400 ZERO_SUMMON_LIMIT); clear error notification
- Backend: combatEntities.js POST accepts + persists all group/summon fields;
  CombatEntity schema already updated (group + summon sub-fields)
- Kill scoreboard: counts each group member killed individually
- Cache bump v=20260310l → v=20260310m

commit.message:
Add persistent JSON config for BI Scanner settings

BI Scanner settings now saved to /tmp/alpha_bi_config.json and
survive app reboots. Includes: direction, threshold, auto_enabled,
scan times (h/m for scan 1 and 2), cache_ttl_h.

Settings auto-save when user changes any value. Loads defaults
if no config file exists. Same pattern as Biotech Scanner config.
